svg.aria-label: Ocean depth metaphor showing fast-changing surface tech hype and stable mathematical foundations down to physics
svg.text: What changes every week vs. what hasn't changed in 40 years
↑ All of this changes every 3 months — somehow always "best practice" ↑
Quantization formats · Weight loading · Threadpool scheduling · Memory arena
AVX2 · AVX-512 · AMX · SSE2 · VNNI · Cache-line alignment · Loop tiling
Changes: when Intel/AMD ship new ISA extensions (~5 year cycles)
L1/L2/L3 cache · DRAM bandwidth · NUMA topology · Prefetch patterns
Changes: the physics of caches haven't changed since the 1960s
⛰ Bedrock Mathematics — Literally Does Not Change
Matrix multiply · Softmax · Layer norm · Attention · Backpropagation
Calculus (1665) · Linear algebra (1848) · Backprop (1986) · Attention (2017) — that's the whole stack
Translate C to machine code · optimization passes · register allocation · vectorization
x86 ISA · instruction encoding · register files · pipeline stages · µops
Execution units · cache SRAM · interconnect · power delivery
⚛ Physics — The Absolute Source of Truth
Electromagnetism · Quantum mechanics · Thermodynamics · Speed of light · Conservation laws
The deeper you build, the longer it lasts. Unless physics is wrong, this stack holds.
